judge.purpose: Grade one route-level copy pack or canonical copy-approval package for build readiness, never predicted lift
judge.decision: accept the control as a test-ready candidate, improve it, or fail the copy stage

insufficient_evidence_when: reader language or objection is asserted without supplied context
a consequential claim lacks visible approved evidence
the action consequence or destination is unavailable

deterministic_gates.description: Copy pack matches its typed contract and every page copy slot exactly once
Promise and proof units reference copy-approved claims; prohibited persuasion patterns and unresolved truth review fail
Actions reference declared interactions and material terms, consequences, reversibility, and recovery are explicit
Channel reader jobs, all page states, variants, and comprehension checks are complete

criteria.name: Reader and situation fit
criteria.definition: The control uses legitimate reader context, vocabulary, awareness, intent, emotion, and objections to make the message relevant without invented familiarity or covert personalization
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite the copy span and corresponding reader-contract field

anchors.below_bar: The copy addresses the wrong reader or relies on invented pain, familiarity, or sensitive personalization

7.0.state: The intended reader can recognize the situation
7.0.exclusion: Vocabulary or objection handling remains generic

7.5.state: Situation and major objection are recognizable

8.0.state: Language fits the reader's awareness and intent
8.0.delta_from_previous: Aligns the message with current knowledge and purpose
8.0.exclusion: Emotion or stakes are handled superficially

8.5.state: The message respects the reader's actual emotional and decision context
8.5.exclusion: One material objection lacks a credible response

9.0.state: Reader context and objections govern the complete control
9.0.delta_from_previous: Integrates the remaining objection without derailing priority
9.0.exclusion: One line still sounds like seller-centered generic copy

9.5.state: Every material line feels legitimately specific to this reader and moment
9.5.delta_from_previous: Removes the final generic or seller-centered unit
9.5.exclusion: Any fit decision could be made more precise from supplied context

10.0.state: Reference-quality reader fit without invented intimacy
10.0.delta_from_previous: Eliminates the last avoidable contextual ambiguity
10.0.exclusion: An expert reader researcher would change any material fit decision

criteria.name: Message priority and comprehension
criteria.definition: The control presents the most decision-relevant information first and makes meaning, mechanism, proof limits, and consequence accurately understandable
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite the attention order and smallest ambiguous or decisive span

anchors.below_bar: The main meaning, offer, status, or consequence is materially confusing

7.5.state: Primary message and action are easy to find
7.5.exclusion: Mechanism or proof relationship is unclear

8.0.state: Value, mechanism, and action form a coherent sequence
8.0.delta_from_previous: Connects how the product works to the next action
8.0.exclusion: One qualification or consequence is buried

8.5.state: Qualifications and consequences appear where the decision needs them
8.5.exclusion: A likely paraphrase or expectation error remains

9.0.state: The intended message survives scanning and accurate paraphrase
9.0.exclusion: One transition or hierarchy choice requires rereading

9.5.state: Meaning, priority, proof limits, and consequence are immediately coherent
9.5.exclusion: Any supplied context could clarify the message further

10.0.delta_from_previous: Removes the last avoidable interpretive ambiguity
10.0.exclusion: A senior content designer would change any material hierarchy decision

criteria.name: Specificity and credibility
criteria.definition: The control uses concrete, supportable language whose strength, qualification, mechanism, source, and limits match the approved claim evidence
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite the exact claim span and its claim-ledger support or missing evidence
critical_failure_conditions: material unsupported, exaggerated, stale, fabricated, or misleading claim
invented customer language, number, testimonial, urgency, scarcity, guarantee, capability, or evidence

anchors.below_bar: A material claim is unsupported, misleading, or stronger than its evidence

7.0.state: Material claims avoid fabrication and major exaggeration
7.0.exclusion: Important language remains abstract or weakly qualified

7.5.state: Claims are qualified at a basic credible level

8.0.state: Claims name concrete mechanisms or observable behavior
8.0.exclusion: Proof placement or evidence strength is uneven

8.5.state: Claim strength and adjacent proof generally match
8.5.exclusion: One material limit or source relationship is implicit

9.0.state: Every material claim exposes mechanism, support, and relevant limits
9.0.delta_from_previous: Makes the remaining source or limit explicit
9.0.exclusion: One phrase could imply more than the evidence supports

9.5.state: The control is precise, concrete, and fully proportionate to visible evidence
9.5.exclusion: Any factual unit could be more exact from supplied evidence

10.0.delta_from_previous: Eliminates the final avoidable evidentiary ambiguity
10.0.exclusion: A senior evidence reviewer would change any material claim

criteria.name: Honest motivation
criteria.definition: The control connects legitimate product value to progress the reader already wants while preserving free evaluation and avoiding fear, shame, pressure, or manufactured urgency
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite the motivating span and the reader progress it serves

anchors.below_bar: The copy coerces, shames, threatens, or manufactures motivation

7.0.state: The copy names a legitimate reason to act

7.5.state: Value connects to a recognizable reader goal
7.5.exclusion: Effort, risk, or choice is minimized

8.0.state: Motivation acknowledges material effort and choice
8.0.exclusion: One line over-pressures or overstates stakes

8.5.state: The complete control supports action without pressure
8.5.exclusion: The value could connect more directly to desired progress

9.0.state: Value, effort, and agency form a credible reason to act
9.0.exclusion: One motivational unit is redundant or emotionally accidental

9.5.state: Every motivating unit advances informed, self-directed choice
9.5.delta_from_previous: Removes the last redundant or accidental pressure
9.5.exclusion: Any motivation choice could better respect supplied reader context

10.0.delta_from_previous: Eliminates the final avoidable agency tension
10.0.exclusion: A senior behavior or ethics reviewer would change any material choice

criteria.name: Action and consequence clarity
criteria.definition: Controls, confirmations, errors, and recovery copy predict the destination, effort, commitment, reversibility, safety, and next system behavior
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite the action or state copy and corresponding interaction consequence
critical_failure_conditions: a consequential action, material term, recovery path, or reversibility condition is misleading or hidden

anchors.below_bar: A consequential action or recovery path is materially ambiguous or deceptive

7.0.state: The primary action predicts its basic outcome
7.0.delta_from_previous: Clarifies the main destination or change
7.0.exclusion: Effort, commitment, or recovery remains vague

7.5.state: Primary outcome and main recovery are clear
7.5.delta_from_previous: Adds the largest missing recovery cue
7.5.exclusion: A material consequence or safe state is implicit

8.0.state: Actions state material consequence and what remains safe
8.0.delta_from_previous: Makes the remaining safety or consequence detail explicit
8.0.exclusion: Reversibility or next system behavior is unclear

8.5.state: Reversibility and next system behavior are explicit
8.5.exclusion: One edge state loses information scent

9.0.state: Normal and edge states preserve orientation, agency, and recovery
9.0.exclusion: One label could predict its result more precisely

9.5.state: Every action and state precisely communicates outcome, effort, and recovery
9.5.exclusion: Any action decision could be clearer from supplied behavior

10.0.delta_from_previous: Removes the last avoidable behavioral ambiguity
10.0.exclusion: A senior content designer would change any consequential action copy

criteria.name: Voice and economy
criteria.definition: The control expresses the approved brand point of view with stable terminology and the least language that preserves meaning, proof, accessibility, and confidence
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite the smallest voice, terminology, rhythm, or redundancy span

anchors.below_bar: The copy is tonally inappropriate, imitative, inaccessible, or bloated enough to obstruct meaning

7.0.state: Tone is usable and terminology mostly stable
7.0.delta_from_previous: Removes the primary tonal or terminology failure
7.0.exclusion: Generic AI cadence or repetition remains

7.5.state: Voice is consistent and major repetition is removed
7.5.exclusion: The brand point of view remains faint

8.0.state: Vocabulary and rhythm express the brand without obscuring meaning
8.0.delta_from_previous: Adds a recognizable but restrained point of view
8.0.exclusion: One flourish or setup does not earn attention

8.5.state: Every major unit earns attention and tone fits its state
8.5.delta_from_previous: Removes the remaining decorative or misplaced flourish
8.5.exclusion: A line is longer or less stable than necessary

9.0.state: Voice, terminology, and economy reinforce the complete message
9.0.exclusion: One phrase could be more distinctive without losing clarity

9.5.state: The control is recognizably authored, stable, and maximally economical for the decision
9.5.exclusion: Any word-level choice could improve clarity or character from supplied context

10.0.delta_from_previous: Eliminates the final avoidable word or tonal mismatch
10.0.exclusion: A senior copy lead would change any material language choice

aggregation.method: weighted mean over anchor numeric values
aggregation.critical_gate: any critical below_bar or deterministic gate failure fails the copy pack regardless of weighted score
aggregation.rounding: retain raw score to two decimals; display nearest 0.5 with ties away from zero
aggregation.missing_evidence: criterion insufficient_evidence; overall abstains when decision-critical

output_contract.repair_scope: contract shape only; preserve semantic anchors and evidence meaning

thresholds: predeclare from decision costs and independent human agreement before opening holdout
calibration.boundary_pairs: required for every adjacent anchor before promotion
calibration.holdout_policy: Do not call the judge accurate or promoted until a locked holdout clears predeclared thresholds

provenance.runtime: clean-context Pi JSON subprocess with no tools; copy context uses a digest-bound page-copy-v1 projection

changelog.motivation: establish a copy-specific pretest rubric without claiming lift
changelog.expected_effect: separate reader, message, truth, motivation, action, and voice quality while preserving deterministic truth and agency gates
changelog.evidence: structurally complete but not independently calibrated

changelog.motivation: share the corrected typed verdict envelope and bounded contract-repair behavior with copy judging
changelog.expected_effect: prevent malformed evidence fields from bypassing or aborting copy evaluation
changelog.evidence: deterministic contract tests; copy judge remains uncalibrated

changelog.motivation: use the same lossless mechanical normalization before copy-judge model repair
changelog.expected_effect: preserve verdict meaning while avoiding a redundant reformatting call
changelog.evidence: deterministic malformed-verdict replay; copy judge remains uncalibrated

changelog.motivation: bind judgments to the prompt package that adds exact model-availability preflight
changelog.expected_effect: fail before expensive generation when a configured model route is unavailable; semantic copy criteria and anchors are unchanged
changelog.evidence: controller and prompt-contract tests; no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: bind copy judgments to the prompt package containing stage-scoped planning evaluation
changelog.expected_effect: no copy-criterion change; preserve exact prompt-package provenance
changelog.evidence: structural validation required; no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: live E2E evidence showed high-thinking Sol judging and full upstream context dominated per-page latency
changelog.expected_effect: use Terra medium and a digest-bound relevant context projection for copy throughput without changing semantic criteria
changelog.evidence: controller tests and a new live E2E are required; no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: bind copy evaluation to the canonical build-approval prompt and truthful performance boundary
changelog.expected_effect: allow exact page copy to be build-approved without implying conversion proof
changelog.evidence: deterministic contract and end-to-end fixture validation required; no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: full copy-approval re-emission made Luna spend minutes reproducing unchanged controls
changelog.expected_effect: bind copy evaluation to patch-oriented approval while preserving deterministic full-copy assembly
changelog.evidence: timed live regression and controller tests required; semantic criteria unchanged and no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: bind copy evaluation to the prompt package that treats competitor pages as untrusted evidence and forbids borrowed competitor copy
changelog.expected_effect: preserve copy criteria while hardening provenance and prompt-injection boundaries
changelog.evidence: controller fixtures and adversarial prompt tests required; no calibration labels added
